pi_items.source: task.json line 5 (task description step 1), task.json line 17 (metadata), export_result.sh line 17, verifier.py line 43. Verified against Drupal Commerce documentation: https://drupal-commerce.readthedocs.io/en/latest/v2/recipes/orders.html - Order Item Types are valid Drupal Commerce configuration entities. Machine name 'service' follows Drupal naming conventions (lowercase, no spaces).

task.json line 5 (task description step 2), task.json line 18 (metadata), export_result.sh lines 20-23, verifier.py line 69. Verified against Drupal field naming conventions: https://www.hashbangcode.com/article/drupal-9-selecting-machine-names-content-entities-and-fields - Field follows proper Drupal conventions with 'field_' prefix and underscores for word separation, within 32 character limit.

task.json line 5 (task description step 4), task.json line 19 (metadata), export_result.sh line 30, verifier.py line 87. Verified against Drupal Commerce Product Type documentation: https://docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/developer-guide/products/product-information-structure/ - Product Types are configuration bundle entities in Drupal Commerce. Machine name 'service' follows Drupal naming conventions.

task.json line 5 (task description step 5), task.json line 20 (metadata), export_result.sh line 34, verifier.py lines 104 and 109. iPhone is a real Apple product. Screen repair is a real service category. Verified against market data: https://www.ifixit.com/Wiki/iPhone_Screen_Repair and https://homeguide.com/costs/iphone-screen-repair-cost - iPhone screen repair is a well-established service category. This is a synthetic product title but accurately represents a real-world service.

task.json line 5 (task description step 5), task.json line 21 (metadata), export_result.sh line 33, verifier.py referenced indirectly. SKU follows typical e-commerce naming conventions: 'SVC' prefix for Service product type, 'IPHONE' for the device category, 'SCREEN' for the repair type. This is a synthetic internal SKU created for the Drupal Commerce database, not an official Apple or repair shop SKU.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires setting up a customizable service product architecture in Drupal Commerce. The agent must create a custom Order Item Type named 'service' with a required Device Serial Number field (field_device_serial_number) of type Text (plain), configure it to appear in the Add to Cart form display, create a Product Type named 'service' linked to this Order Item Type, and create a service product titled 'iPhone Screen Repair' with SKU 'SVC-IPHONE-SCREEN' and price $89.99 USD. All metadata values are verified to be internally consistent with the task description, valid Drupal/Drupal Commerce naming conventions, and technically sound. The product data is synthetic but based on real-world iPhone repair services which typically cost $80-$250 for third-party repairs.
